REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS, THAT PLAINTIFF WAS A TENANT. PLAINTIFF SHALL FURTHER ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. IF PLAINTIFF IS ASSERTING ANY CLAIMS INDEPENDENT OF BEING A TENANT, PLAINTIFF MUST SO CLEARLY STATE. IF ALL PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE OUT OF PURPORTED TENANCY AND ITS IMPROPER TERMINATION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING THE EXISTENCE OF TENANCY (AS OPPOSED TO LICENSE, EMPLOYMENT, ETC). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY SHIFTED ITS BURDEN. OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WITH COMPETENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF'S LATE-FILED OPPOSITION WAS CONSIDERED AND STRICKEN. PLAINTIFF WAS PROVIDED WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF CITING AUTHORITIES RE: LATE-FILED BRIEF. NO RELEVANT AUTHORITIES CITED. PLAINTIFF ALSO FILED DECLARATIONS AND AFFIDAVITS ALONG WITH A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF. AFFIDAVITS AND DECLARATIONS HAVE NOT BEEN AUTHORIZED AND ARE STRICKEN. TO THE EXTENT, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ASSERTS SOME KIND OF ATTORNEY FAULT RELIEF, IT IS NOT PROPERLY BEFORE THE COURT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F, HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

John F. Lee; 415-863-4336; appearing as counsel for plaintiff/cross-defendant Andrew Kangas who is also appearing.
Samuel C. Ray; 415-579-1414; Colla & Ray LLP; counsel for defendant appearing via Zoom.

Mr. Lee contests the courts tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ESTATE OF CHRISTINA Y.C. CHEN, LEAH PRICE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden. Opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. Plaintiff's late-filed opposition was considered and stricken. Plaintiff was provided with an opportunity to file a supplemental brief citing authorities re: late-filed brief. No relevant authorities cited. Plaintiff also filed declarations and affidavits along with a supplemental brief. Affidavits and declarations have not been authorized and are stricken. To the extent, Plaintiff's counsel asserts some kind of attorney fault relief, it is not properly before the Court.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F, Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Luke Garvey; Colla & Ray LLP; 415-579-1414; appearing for defendants Estate of Christina Y.C. Chen and Leah Chen Price.

No other appearances. Not argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts, not conclusions, that Plaintiff was a tenant. Plaintiff shall further allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action. If Plaintiff is asserting any claims independent of being a tenant, Plaintiff must so clearly state. If all Plaintiff's causes of action arise out of purported tenancy and its improper termination, Plaintiff must allege facts supporting the existence of tenancy (as opposed to license, employment, etc).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
